title: Ultimate Gaming Arena — Sound Effects, Difficulty Levels & Selector Screens

requirements.text: Create a global sound manager module (e.g., frontend/src/hooks/useSoundManager.ts) that uses HTML5 Audio API to play distinct sound effects for: click/button press, win, game over, score increment, and special events. Expose a global mute/unmute toggle that persists its state in localStorage.

quotes: Add sound effects for clicks, wins, game over, scoring, and special events.
Include a sound toggle button (Mute / Unmute).

acceptanceCriteria: Distinct sounds play for click, win, game over, score, and special events across all games.
A mute/unmute toggle button is visible and functional in all game pages.
Mute state persists across page refreshes via localStorage.
No sound plays when muted.

requirements.text: Create a reusable DifficultySelector component styled in the dark neon theme that presents Easy / Medium / Hard options and a game title. This screen must appear before each game starts for Tic Tac Toe (SinglePlayer), Snake, and Traffic Car. The selected difficulty is passed to the game logic.

quotes: Add difficulty selector screen before starting each game.
📋 Difficulty Selector Screen add to all games
Pre-game screen before each game launches, styled in the dark neon theme

acceptanceCriteria: A full-screen or overlay difficulty selector appears when navigating to Tic Tac Toe (SinglePlayer), Snake, and Traffic Car pages before gameplay begins.
Selector shows Easy, Medium, and Hard options with neon styling consistent with the rest of the app.
Selecting a difficulty dismisses the selector and starts the game at the chosen difficulty.
Returning to the selector (e.g., restart) re-shows the difficulty screen.

requirements.text: Upgrade SinglePlayer Tic Tac Toe (frontend/src/pages/SinglePlayer.tsx) with three AI difficulty modes: Easy uses fully random moves; Medium randomly mixes random and optimal moves (e.g., 50/50); Hard uses Minimax algorithm for unbeatable AI. The difficulty is set from the DifficultySelector screen.

quotes: Tic Tac Toe: Easy (Random AI), Medium (Mixed AI), Hard (Minimax AI)

acceptanceCriteria: Easy AI makes fully random valid moves.
Medium AI alternates or randomly mixes random and Minimax moves.
Hard AI uses Minimax and never loses.
Difficulty label is shown during gameplay (e.g., badge or subtitle).
Win/lose/draw sound effects play on respective outcomes.

requirements.text: Upgrade the Snake game (frontend/src/pages/SnakeGame.tsx) with three speed-based difficulty modes: Easy uses slow tick interval (~200ms), Medium uses normal (~130ms), Hard uses fast (~70ms). Difficulty is set from the DifficultySelector screen.

quotes: Snake: Easy (Slow speed), Medium (Normal speed), Hard (Fast speed)

acceptanceCriteria: Easy mode runs at a visibly slow snake speed.
Medium mode runs at the current default speed.
Hard mode runs noticeably faster than Medium.
Difficulty label is displayed during gameplay.
Sound effects play on food eaten (score), game over, and new high score.

requirements.text: Upgrade the Traffic Car game (frontend/src/pages/TrafficCarGame.tsx) with three difficulty modes: Easy uses slow traffic speed and fewer cars; Medium uses normal speed and car density; Hard uses fast speed with more simultaneous cars. Difficulty is set from the DifficultySelector screen.

quotes: Traffic Car: Easy (Slow traffic), Medium (Normal), Hard (Fast + more cars)

acceptanceCriteria: Easy mode has visibly slower and sparser traffic.
Medium mode matches current default behavior.
Hard mode has faster and denser traffic from the start.
Difficulty label is displayed during gameplay.
Sound effects play on collision (game over), scoring milestones, and special events.

requirements.text: Integrate the global mute/unmute toggle button into all game pages and the Home page header/navbar area, styled consistently with the dark neon theme (e.g., a speaker icon with neon glow).

quotes: Include a sound toggle button (Mute / Unmute)

acceptanceCriteria: Mute/unmute button is visible on all game pages.
Button shows a clear muted vs. unmuted visual state.
Button uses neon styling consistent with the app theme.
Toggling mute immediately silences or restores all game sounds.

constraints: All sound effects must use HTML5 Audio API — no Web Audio API oscillators for these new effects (existing Web Audio usage in TrafficCarGame/RockPaperScissors may remain).
Dark neon theme must remain consistent across all new UI elements.
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Backend (backend/main.mo) must not be changed — all game logic remains client-side.
nonGoals: Two-player Tic Tac Toe difficulty levels (difficulty only applies to single-player AI mode).
Online multiplayer or backend score persistence.
New games beyond the existing six.
Difficulty levels for Rock Paper Scissors, Memory Card Match, or Snake and Ladder.
Custom sound file uploads by users.
